skill.name: incident-response-smart-fix
skill.description: Guides structured production incident analysis, root cause investigation, fix implementation, verification, and prevention planning.
skill.summary: Coordinate incident diagnosis, code fixes, testing, and prevention work.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static command, SAM, weak crypto, reconnaissance, and obfuscation findings were reviewed in context. Most are false positives from Markdown fences, placeholder labels, git terminology, and incident-response examples, but the skill can guide shell diagnostics and production code changes.

medium_findings.title: Repository Script Execution Guidance
medium_findings.description: The playbook recommends git bisect with a reproduction script. This is legitimate debugging guidance, but running repository scripts can execute untrusted project code.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is explicitly present and can execute a local script. It appears defensive and diagnostic, not malicious.

medium_findings.title: Sensitive Incident Data Handling
medium_findings.description: The workflow asks users to analyze logs, stack traces, observability links, traces, and user impact. These inputs can contain secrets or personal data if users do not redact them.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The requested inputs are normal for incident response and commonly include sensitive operational data. No exfiltration path was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fences Reported as Backtick Execution
low_findings.description: Most external command findings are Markdown prompt and expected-output fences. They are documentation delimiters, not Ruby or shell backtick execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged backticks are Markdown formatting around examples and file names. No Ruby code or command substitution syntax is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Sensitive and Weak Crypto Keywords
low_findings.description: The SAM and weak cryptography alerts correspond to placeholders such as SECURITY_SCAN, SECURITY_CONCERNS, git SHA references, and fix-design text. No Windows SAM access or weak crypto implementation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are output labels for review reports. They do not reference credential stores, password dumping, hashing code, or cryptographic algorithms.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Language
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alerts are incident workflow examples, review checklist items, and database performance contexts. They do not instruct unauthorized network scanning or host discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are about tests, database query performance, and minimal code changes. No scanning target, enumeration command, or offensive workflow is present.

low_findings.title: Non-Instructional Extended Thinking Text
low_findings.description: The skill contains long bracketed Extended thinking text. It is poor marketplace content, but it does not contain override, skip-review, or authority-claim prompt injection instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text describes the workflow and does not attempt to change evaluator instructions. It should be cleaned, but it is not confirmed malicious.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Script Execution Through Git Bisect
dangerous_patterns.description: The documented git bisect flow runs a local reproduction script. Users should inspect scripts and run them in a controlled environment before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is directly documented and can run arbitrary local project code. The context is legitimate debugging.

content.user_title: Resolve Production Incidents With Smart Fixes
content.value_statement: Production incidents need structured diagnosis, not rushed patches. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through analysis, fixes, tests, and prevention.
actual_capabilities: Structures incident triage around error signatures, stack traces, logs, and user impact.
Guides root cause analysis with code review, dependency checks, and git bisect planning.
Routes fix work by technology area, including Python, TypeScript, Go, Rust, database, and security tasks.
Defines verification steps for tests, performance checks, security review, and rollout readiness.
Provides prevention planning for documentation, monitoring, static analysis, and postmortem follow-up.
limitations: Does not connect directly to Sentry, DataDog, OpenTelemetry, or other observability systems.
Requires users to provide logs, traces, reproduction steps, and repository context.
May suggest shell commands or project scripts that must be reviewed before execution.
Does not replace human approval for production deployment or incident command decisions.

use_cases.title: Triage a Production Error
use_cases.description: Organize logs, traces, frequency, affected users, and recent deployments into a root cause investigation plan.

use_cases.title: Implement a Verified Hotfix
use_cases.description: Turn an identified root cause into a minimal code change with regression tests and rollout checks.

use_cases.title: Prevent Repeat Incidents
use_cases.description: Convert an incident resolution into monitoring, lint rules, documentation, and postmortem follow-up.

prompt_templates.title: Start Incident Triage
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to triage this incident. I will provide the error message, affected service, recent deployments, and known user impact.

prompt_templates.title: Find the Root Cause
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to investigate the root cause. Review these logs, stack traces, reproduction steps, and related code paths.

prompt_templates.title: Plan the Fix
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create a minimal fix plan. Include files to inspect, tests to add, rollout risk, and rollback steps.

prompt_templates.title: Run Full Verification
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill for comprehensive verification. Include regression tests, performance checks, security review, monitoring, and prevention actions.

output_examples.input: Checkout requests started timing out after the latest deployment.
output_examples.output: The response identifies the error signature, likely deployment correlation, candidate code paths, missing metrics, and a staged investigation plan.

output_examples.input: A login endpoint security scan reports SQL injection risk.
output_examples.output: The response proposes parameterized queries, input validation, security regression tests, rollout controls, and documentation updates.

output_examples.input: A frontend TypeError affects Safari users after an API change.
output_examples.output: The response separates frontend null handling, backend contract correction, cross-browser tests, and API documentation changes.

best_practices: Redact secrets, tokens, personal data, and customer records before sharing incident artifacts.
Review any suggested shell command or project script before running it.
Use the workflow with human approval for production changes and rollout decisions.
anti_patterns: Do not paste raw production logs that contain secrets or personal data.
Do not run reproduction scripts from untrusted repositories without inspection.
Do not treat the generated fix plan as deployment approval.

faq.question: Does this skill automatically fix production systems?
faq.answer: No. It provides structured guidance for analysis, implementation, testing, and rollout review.

faq.question: Can it use observability tools directly?
faq.answer: No. Users must provide relevant Sentry, DataDog, OpenTelemetry, or log details.

faq.question: Is it safe for sensitive incidents?
faq.answer: Use caution. Redact secrets, personal data, and customer records before sharing incident context.

faq.question: Why is the audit risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill can suggest diagnostic commands and code changes, even though no malicious behavior was found.

faq.question: Does it support postmortems?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes documentation, prevention, monitoring, and knowledge base follow-up steps.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
